title: Lagenocarpus clarkei
intro: Lagenocarpus clarkei, also known as Clarke's water sedge, is a type of aquatic plant belonging to the family Cyperaceae, commonly found in tropical regions. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. Bioactive constituents present in Lagenocarpus clarkei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to support immune function and alleviate various health conditions.
uses: Lagenocarpus clarkei has been reported to have various medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in folk medicine to treat fever and other health issues.
